description: You are a medical physicist calibrating a raw CT scan slice. Calibrate the 16-bit grayscale detector data in '~/Fiji_Data/raw/ct/patient_ct_raw.tif' to Hounsfield Units (HU) using Air (-1000 HU) and Water (0 HU) as reference points. Segment the skull bone tissue using a threshold of 400 HU to create a binary mask. Save the calibrated image as 'calibrated_ct.tif', the binary mask as 'bone_mask.png', and a density report as 'density_report.csv' (including Mean_HU for Air, Water, and Bone) to '~/Fiji_Data/results/ct/'. The calibrated image must preserve the full dynamic range.
